info.name: Rockware LiveStore + Graph
info.description: Import this collection with the companion environment `livestore-local.postman_environment.json`. Graph authoring is implemented as oRPC under `{{apiBaseUrl}}/rpc`; request bodies must be wrapped as `{ "json": <input> }` and responses are returned as `{ "json": <output> }`. LiveStore runtime reads are direct HTTP/WebSocket calls under `{{livestoreBaseUrl}}` and `{{livestoreWsUrl}}`. Set `email` and `password`, run Auth / Login, select or set `siteId`, then run Auth / Switch Site before graph requests.

item.name: Auth + Context
item.description: REST endpoints used to establish JWT workspace/site context for graph oRPC calls.

request.description: Saves accessToken, refreshToken, workspaceId, siteId, and userId when available.
Refreshes both access and refresh tokens. Include siteId to force the new JWT into that site context.
Saves the first workspace id to workspaceId if present.

exec: if (body && Array.isArray(body.data) && body.data[0] && body.data[0].id) pm.environment.set('siteId', body.data[0].id);

request.description: Saves the first accessible site id to siteId if present.
Graph procedures require the JWT to include workspaceId and a site the user can access.

item.name: Discovery
item.description: Catalog and hierarchy calls used to find site, station, entity, metric, datasource, and point values needed by graph resolvers.

exec: const gateways = Array.isArray(value) ? value : (value && Array.isArray(value.gateways) ? value.gateways : []);

request.description: Returns gateways with assigned datasources. Saves the first datasource id to datasourceId when present.

exec: if (body && Array.isArray(body.data) && body.data[0] && body.data[0].id) pm.environment.set('datasourceId', body.data[0].id);

request.description: Saves the first datasource id to datasourceId if present.

exec: const points = Array.isArray(body) ? body : (body && Array.isArray(body.data) ? body.data : []);

request.description: Saves the first point id to pointId for tag resolver examples.

System entity keys include imm.site, imm.workcenter, imm.station, imm.job, imm.product, imm.material, imm.tool, imm.customer, imm.order, imm.workOrder, imm.employee, imm.shiftInstance, imm.statusReason, and imm.statusCategory.

exec: if (value && Array.isArray(value.data) && value.data[0] && value.data[0].id) pm.environment.set('entityInstanceId', value.data[0].id);
if (pm.environment.get('entityCatalogKey') === 'imm.station' && value && Array.isArray(value.data) && value.data[0] && value.data[0].id) pm.environment.set('stationId', value.data[0].id);

request.description: Set entityCatalogKey to imm.station to capture stationId from the first result.
Metric entityType values: STATION, WORKCENTER, SITE, JOB. Granularities: MINUTE, HOUR, SHIFT, DAY, LIVE.

item.name: Graph Types (oRPC)
item.description: Graph type catalog and site-authored type CRUD. Integration type refs use @namespace/key, for example @imm/station.

request.description: Will fail with GRAPH_TYPE_HAS_NODES if active nodes still use this site graph type.

item.name: Graph Nodes (oRPC)
item.description: Node CRUD and query. Node writes emit graph definition events consumed by LiveStore for hot patching.

request.description: Requires stationId to reference an existing imm.station entity. Built-in @imm/station fields are materialized as properties.

exec: if (value && Array.isArray(value.data) && value.data[0] && Array.isArray(value.data[0].properties) && value.data[0].properties[0]) pm.environment.set('graphPropertyId', value.data[0].properties[0].id);

item.name: Query Nodes By Type Facets Properties

request.description: Returns requestedProperties keyed by requested property key when properties are supplied.
Fails when node properties have external dependents or active hooks.

item.name: Graph Properties (oRPC)
item.description: Property resolver CRUD. Supported resolverType values are tag, metric, entity, expr, window, and rollup.

request.description: LiveStore subscribes to tags.<deviceId>.<tagPath> for tag properties. datasourceId and pointId are the expected local values.
Expressions reference dependencies as p_<property_uuid_with_underscores>. The Create Tag Property script sets graphSourcePropertySymbol.
Rollup membership is resolved by LiveStore from the domain hierarchy at runtime. Optional resolver.parent can be { model, id }.
Dry-run resolver validation and dependency extraction without persistence.
Fails when the property has active dependents or is used by active hooks.

item.name: Graph Hooks (oRPC)
item.description: Hooks emit LiveStore NATS events when property conditions match. They do not produce graph values.

request.description: Current catalog includes livestore.hook_triggered@1 and imm.cycle_completed@1.
imm.cycle_completed requires stationId context. Bind it to a string-valued property in real usage.

item.name: LiveStore Runtime
item.description: Direct LiveStore service calls. These expose the runtime snapshot and readiness, not graph authoring writes.

request.description: Returns runtime nodes with properties and current ValueEnvelope values. Saves livestoreNodeId and livestorePropertyId from the first result.

item.name: LiveStore WebSocket
item.description: Postman can open WebSocket requests in recent versions. If this imports as a plain GET, create a new WebSocket request manually and paste `{{livestoreWsUrl}}/graph/live`. Client messages: `{ "op": "subscribe", "propertyIds": ["{{livestorePropertyId}}"] }` and `{ "op": "unsubscribe", "propertyIds": ["{{livestorePropertyId}}"] }`. Server messages: `{ "op": "value", "propertyId": "...", "envelope": { "value": 18.2, "quality": "good", "timestamp": 1730000000000 } }`.

request.description: Send this after connecting: { "op": "subscribe", "propertyIds": ["{{livestorePropertyId}}"] }. LiveStore sends initial value and updates as { op, propertyId, envelope }.
